channel.title: Quick Take on CodetoCore
channel.description: Recent content in Quick Take on CodetoCore

item.title: TF-M vs TF-A — When Do You Reach For Which?
item.description: TF-M is for Cortex-M microcontrollers, TF-A is for Cortex-A application processors. Different exception models, different attack surfaces, not interchangeable.

item.title: Do I Need a Secure Element If My MCU Has a Crypto Accelerator?
item.description: Short answer: they solve different problems. A crypto accelerator makes crypto operations fast. A secure element makes sure your keys never leave the chip.

item.title: Is SysTick Accurate Enough for Microsecond Timing?
item.description: Short answer: the counter is. Everything around it usually isn't.

item.title: Why Does My Code Work in Debug But Break in Release?
item.description: Short answer: optimization isn't doing the wrong thing. Your code was always wrong — debug builds just happened to hide it.

item.title: What Actually Happens Before main() Runs?
item.description: A lot. The CPU walks through six setup stages between power-up and your first line of C — and bugs in any of them look very different from each other.
